name: Tomatillo Chicken Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Coarsely shredded chicken or turkey
recipeInstructions:
Chopped cilantro, tomatillo slices, and lime slices for garnish
In a large bowl, mix chicken, 2 cups cheese, chilies, and oregano. Season with salt and pepper; set aside.
In a large skillet, heat ½ inch oil over medium-high heat. When oil is hot, add one tortilla at a time; cook, turning once, just until limp (about 10 seconds). Drain on paper towels. While tortillas are warm, spoon about ¼ to ½ cup chicken mixture down the center of each; roll tortillas to form enchilada. Set enchiladas seam side down in a 9-by-13-inch baking dish or medium-sized baking dishes. (At this point, enchiladas may be covered and refrigerated until the next day.)
Cover enchiladas with foil and bake in a 350°F oven for 15 minutes until hot in the center (cook for 30 minutes if they were refrigerated). Uncover and top with remaining cheese. Bake, uncovered, until cheese melts, about 10 minutes.
To serve, spoon warm tomatillo sauce over each plate. Place enchiladas on top of sauce. Spoon sour cream over enchiladas and garnish.
Tomatillo Sauce
1/3 cup salad oil
2 medium onions, chopped
1 to 2 (4-oz) cans chopped green chilies
1 cup chicken broth
1 (28-oz) can tomatillos
3 T lime juice
2 tsp Dried oregano leaves
2 tsp Sugar
1 tsp Cumin
Salt to taste
Heat oil in a 3 to 4 quart pan over medium-high heat. Add onions and cook, stirring often, until soft, about 10 minutes. Stir in green chilies, chicken broth, tomatillos, lime juice, oregano, sugar, and cumin. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, for 25 minutes. Whirl sauce in a blender until smooth. Season with salt. Serves 6 to 9.
